content.speakerText: Which social media feature do you think is the most useful, and why?
content.speakerTextCn: 你认为哪个社交媒体功能最有用？为什么？
expectedAnswers: I think Stories are the most useful because they feel personal and disappear after 24 hours.
DMs—they let me chat privately with friends without posting publicly.
Reels for discovery; I’ve found so many small businesses through trending videos.
evaluationCriteria: 是否明确指出一个具体功能（如Stories, DMs, Reels）
是否给出合理且具体的理由（使用场景、情感、实用性）
是否使用了场景相关词汇（discovery, private, trending, authentic）

content.scenarioHint: 你和朋友在讨论社交媒体App的功能，你想了解对方为什么使用某个特定功能，比如‘故事’或‘动态贴纸’。
content.speakerText: Why do you use Stories instead of just posting regular photos?
content.speakerTextCn: 你为什么用‘故事’功能而不是直接发普通照片呢？
expectedAnswers: I like how they disappear after 24 hours—it feels more casual.
They’re perfect for behind-the-scenes stuff, not just polished pics.
I use them to stay connected without overwhelming my feed.
Regular posts feel too formal—I’d rather share quick moments.
evaluationCriteria: 意图达成度：是否明确询问了对方使用该功能的原因
语言准确性：语法正确，时态和用词恰当
表达自然度：符合日常英语对话习惯，不生硬
关键词覆盖：使用了场景关键词如 Stories, regular photos, casual, moments

content.scenarioHint: 你在和同事聊社交媒体工具，想了解对方为何选择使用‘群组聊天’而不是私信来组织活动。
content.speakerText: Why do you prefer group chats over direct messages for planning events?
content.speakerTextCn: 你为什么在组织活动时更喜欢用群聊而不是私信？
expectedAnswers: It’s easier to keep everyone in the loop without repeating myself.
Group chats save time—I don’t have to message each person individually.
I like seeing replies in real time—it’s way more efficient.
DMs get lost, but group chats are visible to all, so no one misses out.
evaluationCriteria: 意图达成度：是否清晰询问了使用群聊而非私信的原因
语言准确性：语法正确，使用了恰当的对比结构（over, instead of）
表达自然度：表达符合职场或日常社交语境，自然流畅
关键词覆盖：使用了场景关键词如 group chats, direct messages, planning, efficiency
